MCP_NOT_FOUND.message: MCP command unavailable or server not registered
MCP_NOT_FOUND.description: An MCP tool/server was referenced but is not enabled or registered in the current session.
MCP_NOT_FOUND.instructions: Enable the MCP server in your opencode.json configuration under the 'mcpServers' key.

RATE_LIMIT.message: API rate limited or quota exceeded
RATE_LIMIT.description: The current API provider returned a rate limit or quota error.
RATE_LIMIT.instructions: Switch to a fallback model or wait for the rate limit window to reset.

ENV_VAR_MISSING.description: A required environment variable is missing from the current shell session.
ENV_VAR_MISSING.instructions: Set the missing environment variable using setx (Windows) or export (Unix).

PLUGIN_CONFLICT.description: Two plugins are providing the same functionality or namespace, causing a conflict.
PLUGIN_CONFLICT.instructions: Uninstall the duplicate plugin via npm uninstall.

MODEL_UNAVAILABLE.description: The requested LLM model is unreachable, timed out, or returned a service error.
MODEL_UNAVAILABLE.instructions: Switch to the next model in the fallback chain.

TOKEN_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.message: Session token budget or context window exceeded
TOKEN_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.description: The current session has exhausted its token budget or exceeded the model's context window.
TOKEN_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.instructions: Start a new session or use distillation to compress context.

SUPERMEMORY_AUTH_FAIL.message: Supermemory API key invalid or missing
SUPERMEMORY_AUTH_FAIL.description: The Supermemory service rejected the API key or it is not configured.
SUPERMEMORY_AUTH_FAIL.instructions: Verify SUPERMEMORY_API_KEY is set correctly in your environment.

GIT_CONFLICT.message: Git merge or rebase conflict detected
GIT_CONFLICT.description: A git operation resulted in merge conflicts that need manual resolution.
GIT_CONFLICT.instructions: Review conflicting files, resolve markers, stage changes, and continue the operation.

PERMISSION_DENIED.message: Permission denied on file or resource access
PERMISSION_DENIED.description: A file system or API operation was rejected due to insufficient permissions.
PERMISSION_DENIED.instructions: Check file ownership/permissions or verify API credentials have required scopes.

PORT_IN_USE.message: Port already in use by another process
PORT_IN_USE.description: The requested network port is already bound by another process.
PORT_IN_USE.instructions: Kill the process using the port or switch to an alternative port.

GEMINI_AUTH_ERROR.description: The Gemini API key is missing, invalid, or not authorized for the requested model.
GEMINI_AUTH_ERROR.instructions: 1. Verify GOOGLE_API_KEY is set in .env (not GOOGLE_API_KEYS). 2. Confirm the key is valid at https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey. 3. In opencode.json provider.google, ensure {env: 'GOOGLE_API_KEY'} (singular). 4. Restart opencode after .env changes.

GEMINI_MODEL_NOT_FOUND.message: Gemini model ID not recognized by the API
GEMINI_MODEL_NOT_FOUND.description: The model ID specified in opencode.json does not match any model available from the Google Generative Language API.
GEMINI_MODEL_NOT_FOUND.instructions: 1. Use exact API model IDs: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3-pro-preview, gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview, gemini-3.1-pro-preview. 2. Avoid invented names like gemini-3-flash (missing -preview suffix) or gemini-2-5-flash (hyphens instead of dots). 3. Run: curl 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models?key=YOUR_KEY' to list valid models.
